metadata.notes: agent_supports: yes; daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready â€” /stats and /help are local-only display commands that never reach the transcript, so no user_message fires and state stays ready.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes; daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

**agent (yes).** Gemini CLI ships a family of built-in slash commands that are handled entirely in the Ink TUI and never round-trip to the model: `/help` (or `/?`) â€” "Display help information about Gemini CLI, including available commands and their usage"; `/stats` â€” "Display detailed statistics for the current Gemini CLI session"; `/about` â€” "Show version info". The bundled CLI marks these `BUILT_IN` command kind (522 occurrences across the chunk-*.js bundle at @google/gemini-cli 0.46.0). They render their output locally; none submits a prompt. This is the direct analogue of claudecode's `/cost` + `/help` pairing (Gemini has no `/cost`, so `/stats` is the equivalent session-info command).

**daemon (full).** The Gemini transport is FilesUnderRoot â€” one append-only JSONL per session under ~/.gemini/tmp/<project>/chats/session-*.jsonl. The parser only emits state-driving events from transcript messages of type `user` (a real text prompt â†’ `user_message` â†’ working) or `gemini` (assistant message; final text-only with no open tool calls â†’ inferred `turn_done` â†’ ready). A synchronous slash command is consumed by the TUI and is NOT persisted to the JSONL transcript as a `user` message, so the parser never sees a line that could open a turn â€” no `user_message`, no `â†’ working`. The session sits in `ready` before, during, and after the slash window. This satisfies both acceptance bullets (state stays ready; no `â†’ working` for the slash command itself).

Note the structural difference from claudecode: claudecode logs a `<local-command>` wrapper into its transcript and relies on `isLocalCommandContent` to filter it. Gemini needs no such filter because it never persists the slash invocation at all â€” there is nothing to drop. The same correct observable (no transition) is reached by a different mechanism.

**driver (ready).** The gemini-cli interactive driver lists `slash` in DRIVE_ELICITS (driver-interactive.sh:87) and dispatches `send|slash` to `send_text` (types text + Enter). The recipe issues `/stats` and `/help` as `slash` steps inside a window held open by `sleep`, and never `wait_turn`s on a slash step. (send_text increments EXPECTED_TURNS even for a slash; that is harmless here because the only `wait_turn` is on the warm-up turn that precedes the slashes â€” no trailing wait_turn can be tripped by the inflated counter.) No stubbed primitive (`keys`, `reset_session`, â€¦) is required.

## Provisional note

daemon=full is grounded in reading the parser plus the documented locality of the commands; the claim that Gemini writes NOTHING to the transcript for a slash invocation is an emission property a doc read can't fully confirm. Confidence held at 0.82; record promotes it from provisional to settled by confirming the transcript carries exactly one user line (the warm-up) across the /stats + /help window.
caveats: Restrict the recipe to genuinely local commands. /chat save|resume, /memory add and /compress (context compaction) DO call the model or mutate session identity and would invalidate the no-LLM premise. /stats, /help, /about are safe.
/clear resets the conversation; avoid it (it belongs to session-reset, not this scenario).
daemon=full rests on Gemini never persisting a slash invocation as a transcript `user` message â€” an emission property record must confirm (provisional; confidence 0.82).

sources.note: /help (or /?): 'Display help information about Gemini CLI, including available commands and their usage'; /stats: 'Display detailed statistics for the current Gemini CLI session'; /about: 'Show version info' â€” all local display commands, no model call.

parseMessage (100-109) only dispatches type=user / type=gemini; parseUser (113-158) opens a turn (user_message â†’ working) only on a real text prompt. A slash command, never persisted as a `user` line, produces no event â€” so no â†’ working.
DRIVE_ELICITS includes `slash` (line 87); send|slash â†’ send_text (line 232). Driver implements every step the recipe needs; no stubbed primitive required.
Confirms transcript shape: only user text prompts and `gemini` messages appear as lines; model is gemini-3-flash-preview.

preconditions: gemini CLI on PATH and authenticated (OAuth or API key)
irrlichd is recording (dev daemon with --record, or isolated mode)
The slash commands chosen (/stats, /help) must remain local-only display commands in the running gemini version
setup: Driver launches `gemini -y` under tmux in a fresh cwd; Gemini mints its own sessionId (harvested from the transcript header)
Avoid /clear (session reset), /chat save|resume, /memory add, /compress (real model calls or identity changes â€” different scenarios)

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: warm

verify: events.jsonl shows the session entering ready at birth and a single readyâ†’workingâ†’ready cycle driven by the warm-up turn ONLY
events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to working or waiting during the /stats + /help window
transcript.jsonl contains exactly 1 user text line (the warm-up) â€” neither /stats nor /help appears as a user turn
